items.q: What determines the energy of a single photon?
items.correct: Its frequency (E = hf) — only frequency, not intensity.
items.misconception: A brighter light has higher-energy photons because intensity makes each photon more energetic.
items.random_wrong: Photon energy depends on how fast the photon is moving relative to the source.
items.why: Students conflate intensity (number of photons) with photon energy (frequency-dependent); this is why dim UV is more dangerous than bright red.

items.q: What is the difference between heat and temperature?
items.correct: Heat is the energy transferred due to temperature difference; temperature measures average kinetic energy of particles.
items.misconception: Heat and temperature are the same thing — both measure how hot something is.
items.random_wrong: Heat is measured in degrees Celsius and temperature in Joules.
items.why: Everyday language uses 'heat' and 'temperature' interchangeably ('turn up the heat'), so students don't distinguish them physically.

items.q: In a balanced nuclear equation, what must be equal on both sides?
items.correct: The total mass number (top numbers) and the total atomic number (bottom numbers) must both balance.
items.misconception: Only the atomic number (proton count) needs to balance.
items.random_wrong: Only the total mass in kilograms needs to balance.
items.why: Students transfer 'balance the atoms' from chemical equations, but in nuclear equations identities can change while nucleon and charge counts are conserved.

items.q: A proton is made of which quarks?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: uud. “udd” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which force is mediated by gluons?
items.why: It's Strong. “EM” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Number of quark generations in the Standard Model:
items.why: Exact answer: 3. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
